title: salix × cernua
intro: Salix × cernua, also known as weeping willow or cleft willow, is a hybrid plant species derived from the Salix genus, which includes willow trees. The Salix × cernua plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The bark and leaves of the Salix × cernua plant offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made from the plant's bark and leaves to leverage its medicinal properties.
